title: Sample Entry — Cell Biology Basics
content: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Cells can be broadly categorized into prokaryotic cells (lacking a nucleus) and eukaryotic cells (containing a membrane-bound nucleus). Key organelles in eukaryotic cells include the mitochondria (energy production), endoplasmic reticulum (protein and lipid synthesis), and the Golgi apparatus (protein modification and sorting).
